sourceMovie.title: Your Name.
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Your Name. works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ocean Waves
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ocean Waves (1993) captures the animation appeal of Your Name. without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts Ocean Waves at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation appeal of Your Name. — that bittersweet edge, the romance — translates cleanly into Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Place Promised in Our Early Days
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Place Promised in Our Early Days (2004) clearly understood what makes animation work — the same quality that keeps Your Name. rewatchable. Expect the same bittersweet rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Anthem of the Heart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Anthem of the Heart (2015) is the kind of film Your Name. fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The animation overlap is real, but it's the bittersweet emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes shares Your Name.'s gift for animation — the 2022 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Your Name. compelling — the romance, the drama — shows up in The Lobster (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Girl Who Leapt Through Time
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation energy of Your Name. is alive in The Girl Who Leapt Through Time (2006), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Belle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Belle (2021) operates in the same animation as Your Name., but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The uplifting payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Quintessential Quintuplets Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Quintessential Quintuplets Movie (2022) belongs to the same conversation as Your Name. — both anchor their animation in romance emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Steins;Gate: The Movie - Load Region of Déjà Vu
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Your Name.'s animation stayed with you, Steins;Gate: The Movie - Load Region of Déjà Vu (2013) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

faq.question: What should I watch after Your Name.?
faq.answer: Try Ocean Waves, Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl, The Place Promised in Our Early Days—each shares DNA with Your Name. while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Your Name.?
faq.answer: Your Name. (2016) is best described as Animation, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Your Name. worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
